paper_title: Pollen Grain Analysis in Castor Bean Genotypes

methods.description: This method involves the collection and morphological characterization of pollen grains from various castor bean genotypes using electron microscopy.

steps.description: Pollen grains from male flowers were collected at anthesis from fifteen castor bean genotypes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect pollen grains from castor bean genotypes

steps.description: The pollen grains were fixed in a modified Karnovsky solution, dehydrated in an ascending ethanol series, and dried in HMDS.
steps.searchable_summary: fix, dehydrate, and dry pollen grains

steps.description: The samples were mounted in metal supports and coated with gold. The images were obtained with a variable pressure scanning electron microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare and image pollen samples using electron microscopy

methods.description: This method measures the dimensions of pollen grains and exine using photomicroscopy.

steps.description: Pollen grains were subjected to weak lactic acetolysis to measure the pollen grains and exine.
steps.searchable_summary: perform lactic acetolysis on pollen grains

steps.description: Images were obtained with a photomicroscope coupled to a Sony camera using the software Image Pro-plus.
steps.searchable_summary: capture images of pollen grains using photomicroscopy

methods.description: This method tests the germination of pollen grains in various culture media under controlled conditions.

steps.description: Pollen grains were inoculated in different culture media with varying concentrations of sucrose, boric acid, calcium nitrate, magnesium sulfate, and potassium nitrate.
steps.searchable_summary: inoculate pollen grains in culture media

steps.description: Petri dishes were kept under controlled temperature conditions in the dark for 24 hours.
steps.searchable_summary: incubate pollen grains under controlled conditions

steps.description: Germinated pollen grains were counted and pollen tube length was measured using a binocular stereomicroscope.
steps.searchable_summary: count germinated pollen grains and measure pollen tube length

steps.description: Calculate the in vitro germination percentage and analyze data using the Scott-Knott test.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate germination percentage and perform statistical analysis

methods.description: This method involves staining pollen grains with acetocarmine and TTC to assess viability.

steps.description: Pollen samples were stained with acetocarmine and TTC, and slides were prepared.
steps.searchable_summary: stain pollen grains with acetocarmine and TTC

steps.description: Determine the amount of viable and nonviable pollen grains per genotype using an optical microscope.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze stained pollen grains for viability

steps.description: Data were subjected to analysis of variance and means were compared by the Scott-Knott test.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and Scott-Knott test on viability data
